qa_pairs.question: What happens if the resident memory (RES) available to Abaqus drops below the Minimum Memory Required (MMR)?
qa_pairs.answer: If the resident memory (RES) dips below the Minimum Memory Required (MMR), Abaqus will exit due to an Out of Memory (OOM) error.

qa_pairs.question: How can one investigate the performance impact of reducing the requested memory for an Abaqus simulation, especially when the Memory Required to Minimize I/O (MRMIO) is large?
qa_pairs.answer: To investigate the performance impact, you can lower the `#SBATCH --mem=` value in your Slurm script, which in turn sets an artificially low value for the `memory=` parameter in the Abaqus command. For example, if your MRMIO is 96GB, you could try running a series of short test jobs with `#SBATCH --mem=8G, 16G, 32G, 64G` until an acceptable minimal performance impact is found.

qa_pairs.question: What is the relationship between reduced resident memory (RES) and scratch space usage in Abaqus simulations?
qa_pairs.answer: When the resident memory (RES) available to Abaqus is significantly reduced below the Memory Required to Minimize I/O (MRMIO), smaller RES values will result in increasingly larger scratch space being used by temporary files.
